summary_text: **Findings** - `[Medium]` `--dry-run` is not strictly read-only in all environments: the sync script always runs `mkdir -p "${target_base}"` before the dry-run guard, so it can create `~/.codex/skills` or `~/.claude/skills` if missing. See [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:113](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh#L113). - `[Low]` Before the explicit `--force` step, the script does not remove/overwrite skill subdirectories because destructive behavior is gated behind `FORCE` and non-dry-run path. See [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:120](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh#L120), [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:132](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh#L132), [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:133](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh#L133). - `[Low]` Your backup/diff preflight commands (`cp` to backup path, `diff`/`git diff`) do not mutate the source local skill directories themselves; they only read sources and write outside them. - `[Low]` If preflight includes `bash tests/test-sync-fugue-local-skills.sh`, it writes only to `mktemp` paths via env overrides, not `~/.codex/skills`/`~/.claude/skills`. See [test-sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:6](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-sync-fugue-local-skills.sh#L6), [test-sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:12](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-sync-fugue-local-skills.sh#L12). **Conclusion** - In your current environment (`~/.codex/skills` and `~/.claude/skills` already exist), the preforce plan is effectively non-destructive for local skill directories. - Strictly speaking, the only potential preforce mutation is base-directory creation by dry-run when those roots are absent.
